message:
style(ffs-cli): revert cargo fmt import-reorder to project-canonical style

Undo the rustfmt import-reorder pass from 37f2649 — the toolchain
that produced it had a different imports_granularity / group_imports
configuration than the project uses on main. The net effect was
shuffling `use anyhow::{...}` / `use super::{...}` / `use
ffs_ondisk::{...}` lists back and forth between two equally valid
orderings, causing ambient fmt diffs in unrelated feature commits.

Restore the style that matches every other crate in the workspace:

- `use anyhow::{Context, Result, bail};` — type re-exports first in
  alphabetical order, then functions/macros.
- `use super::{PRESET_CACHE, PRESET_CONTENTION, ..., PresetMode,
  validate_evidence_args};` — SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE constants first
  in alphabetical order, then CamelCase types, then snake_case
  functions.
- `#[...] use ffs_btrfs::{BTRFS_FS_TREE_OBJECTID, ...,
  BtrfsInodeItem, parse_inode_item, parse_root_item};` — same
  constants-then-types-then-functions pattern.

Extends the same sort back across the rest of the crate:

- crates/ffs-cli/src/cmd_evidence.rs
- crates/ffs-cli/src/cmd_repair.rs
- crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs (largest surface, covers the
  cmd_repair re-exports, asupersync/clap/ffs_* / libc / serde /
  tracing / rustix groups)

Also re-applies the sort to the assert! chain formatting: the
multi-line
    assert!(
        err.to_string()
            .contains("--tail is only supported ..."),
    );
shape is what `cargo fmt` (stable, 1.84) produces for this crate and
is what all other files in ffs-cli use.

No behavioural change. `cargo fmt --check` on the pinned toolchain
should now stay clean without flipping between the two styles.
